seoTitle: Share Editable Draw.io Diagrams Outside Confluence
metaDescription: Export a draw.io diagram from Confluence as an editable PDF, XML file, or shareable link so external clients can open and edit it freely.
intro: When an external client or freelancer needs to edit a draw.io diagram but doesn't have Confluence access, you can still hand off a fully editable copy. This guide covers exporting a diagram in an editable format and sharing it so outside collaborators can open it in a free web-based editor.

steps.heading: Open the diagram for editing
steps.body: Open your Confluence documentation page, click the pencil icon to enter edit mode, and click on your embedded draw.io diagram.

steps.heading: Launch the draw.io editor
steps.body: Click Edit inside the macro frame to open the full draw.io design workspace.

steps.heading: Export as an editable file
steps.body: Go to the File menu, select Export As, and choose the editable PDF or XML format option.

steps.heading: Include editable diagram data
steps.body: Check the box to include a copy of your diagram properties directly inside the exported file so it stays fully editable, not just a flat image.

steps.heading: Or share a cloud link
steps.body: Select the Share button in the header to generate an encrypted, external web sharing link instead of a file.

steps.heading: Send it to your external stakeholder
steps.body: Pass the exported file or link to your outside collaborator so they can open it inside app.diagrams.net.

faq.question: Can external users edit my diagram without a Confluence account?
faq.answer: Yes, exporting as an editable PDF or XML with the diagram data included lets external stakeholders open and edit it in the free app.diagrams.net editor without needing Confluence access.

faq.question: What file format keeps my diagram fully editable?
faq.answer: Editable PDF and XML formats both preserve the diagram's vector data, as long as you check the option to include a copy of the diagram properties during export.

faq.question: Do external collaborators need to buy anything to edit the file?
faq.answer: No, app.diagrams.net is a free public web editor that can open the exported files without a paid account.

faq.question: Will the shapes and arrows stay aligned after export?
faq.answer: They should, but it's worth testing the exported file in an independent viewport to confirm vector shapes and connectors render correctly.
